text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: siera R package ingests CDISC Analysis Results Standard (ARS) metadata (JSON Excel) auto-generates R scripts produce Analysis Results Datasets (ARDs) ADaM datasets. exported user-facing function readARS().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package must always kept CRAN-submittable state. Every change merged main pass R CMD check 0 errors 0 warnings. Avoid adding dependencies, exported symbols, behaviours violate CRAN policies. Long-running environment-specific tests must guarded skip_on_cran().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: commands use R 4.4.1 C:\Program Files\R\R-4.4.1\bin\Rscript.exe. Run single test file filter name: every PR: run document(), load_all(), check(vignettes = FALSE) — must pass 0 errors, 0 warnings. “unable verify current time” NOTE known environment artefact can ignored.
code: devtools::document()          # Regenerate man/ from Roxygen devtools::load_all()          # Load package for interactive dev devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)  # Full R CMD check (Pandoc unavailable locally) devtools::test()              # Run all tests devtools::test_file("tests/testthat/test-readARS.R") devtools::test_file("tests/testthat/test-readARS.R", filter = "4 grouping factors")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: readARS(ARS_path, output_path, adam_path) single entry point: Lopo — list planned outputs Lopa — list planned analyses (maps analyses → outputs) DataSubsets, AnalysisSets, AnalysisGroupings Analyses, AnalysisMethods, AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate, AnalysisMethodCodeParameters .generate_library_code() — static library() calls .generate_program_header() — script banner .generate_adam_loading_code() — readr::read_csv() calls required ADaM datasets .generate_analysis_set_code() — population filter (SAFFL etc.), USUBJID merge analysis dataset ≠ analysis-set dataset .generate_data_subset_code() — clause filters; supports ARS comparators (EQ, NE, , NOTIN, CONTAINS, …) multi-level /nesting .generate_analysis_method_section() — substitutes parameters method code templates; uses assign()/get() caller envir pass operation IDs back Write script — fragments concatenated written ARD_<OutputId>.R output_path.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: JSON/XLSX parity required: every code-generation change must work identically regardless whether ARS input file .json .xlsx. parsers produce normalised tibble names column names; test paths adding new features read tibbles. Code generation string-based: method templates contain literal placeholder strings (e.g. "analysisidhere", "methodidhere") replaced gsub(). Don’t confuse R variables. jsonlite produces nested data frames (list columns) simplifyDataFrame = TRUE. recursive helper .extract_lopa_ids() metadata.R relies structure traverse arbitrary-depth sublist nesting mainListOfContents. analysisId must coerced character binding rows JSON JSON null parses logical NA, causing type conflicts dplyr::bind_rows. Dynamic grouping (AG_vars, AG_var1/2/3…) supports 1–N grouping factors; code generation branches many resultsBy groupings present AnalysisGroupings. n_group_cols determination — number group[n] columns method produces differs num_grp last grouping goes variables= instead =. determined .n_group_cols_from_template() readARS.R, checks valueSource types (by_vars, strata_vars, by_listc) appear parameters whose placeholder tokens actually present method’s code template. Never check parameter table alone — can contain spurious rows whose placeholder tokens absent template (false positives). Never check generated code string — runtime-resolved values won’t match placeholder names. Practical consequence: by_stmt resolves n_group_cols = 0 (group stamping, group1_groupingId etc. ARD). method must emit CDISC-compliant group metadata columns must use by_listc (by_vars/strata_vars) — by_stmt. group_condition_value JSON list-column — jsonlite always returns condition.value list, even single-element EQ arrays. JSON parser must call tidyr::unnest(tmp_AG, cols = "group_condition_value") building tmp_AG expand /NOTIN multi-value conditions (one row per value, group_id repeated) collapse single-value list elements plain character. Use quoted string cols= avoid R CMD check “visible binding” NOTE. _level columns bind_rows type conflicts — cards returns variable_level list--NULLs continuous analyses character categorical ones. combined bind_rows, creates list-type columns containing “NULL” strings. Fix: generate per-df mutate(across(matches("_level$"), vapply(..., function(v) (.null(v)) NA_character_ else .character(v), character(1L)))) block final bind_rows. use across((.list), .character) — converts numeric stat list column character, breaking downstream round() calls. CDISC ARD compliance — ARD row must carry: AnalysisId, operationid, per-resultGroup: group[n]_groupingId + either group[n]_groupId (pre-defined groups, via case_when group[n]_level) group[n]_groupValue (data-driven groupings). See .generate_groupid_code() readARS.R. Empty-data guard — generated method code wrapped (nrow(df2_<analysisid>) != 0){ ... } else { df3_<analysisid> = data.frame(AnalysisId, MethodId, OutputId) } (see .generate_analysis_method_section() AnalysisMethods.R ~L94/L135 readARS.R ~L461). data subset empty cards call skipped stub row (statistics) emitted. correct n/continuous (“data → stats”) root cause #156: 0-event risk difference yields estimate row (→ NA) instead conventional RD = 0. fix must special-case RD methods preserving general guard. Dataset-JSON export (output_format = "datasetjson", issue #160) — appends self-contained block (built .generate_datasetjson_code() R/DatasetJSON.R) generated ARD_<Output>.R. block runs script runtime (generation time) ARD’s column set known sourcing; introspects names(ARD), derives Dataset-JSON column metadata, writes ARD_<Output>.json beside script via optional datasetjson package (Suggests, guarded requireNamespace() emitted code — absent package → message, error). Two runtime gotchas handles: () ARD carries four cards list-columns (stat, fmt_fun, warning, error) must flattened atomic — stat→numeric, rest→character; (b) variable labels use built-dictionary siera + cards vocabulary plus group[n]_(groupingId|groupId|groupValue|level) regex, falling back raw name data-driven ADaM columns. NB: code_pattern block readARS.R dead code — pattern/res/disp never produced; numeric result lives stat list-column. Internal functions prefixed . (e.g. .read_ars_metadata). four symbols exported: readARS, ARS_example, ARD_script_example, %>%.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tidyverse base R: use dplyr, tidyr, stringr; pipe |> (prefer native pipe %>%). cli output: cli::cli_abort() stop(), cli::cli_warn() warning(), cli::cli_inform() message(). Fail fast: check arguments early .na(), nchar(), .null() guards; emit errors pointing bad input. global state: inputs must explicit arguments; reads writes global objects. Function naming: internal helpers prefixed . (e.g. .generate_analysis_set_code()); exported functions camelCase (e.g. readARS(), ARS_example()). Comments: explain , ; section markers end ---- RStudio outline (e.g. # Load ADaMs ----).

title: CLAUDE.md

title: CLAUDE.md
text: gh CLI installed machine. Use GitHub REST API directly via PowerShell instead. Step 1 — retrieve stored token (Git Credential Manager holds GitKraken OAuth token): retrieve reuse session: Step 2 — set headers: Common operations: token OAuth token (gho_ prefix) stored Windows Credential Manager GitKraken. repo scope works standard issue/PR/label operations.
code:
$token = (printf "protocol=https
host=github.com
" | git credential fill | Select-String "^password=").ToString().Replace("password=","").Trim() # in Bash tool printf "protocol=https
host=github.com
" | git credential fill # copy the password= line value $token   = "gho_..."   # from step 1 $headers = @{ Authorization = "token $token"; Accept = "application/vnd.github.v3+json" } $repo    = "clymbclinical/siera" # Create an issue $body = @{ title = "..."; body = "..." } | ConvertTo-Json Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/issues" -Method Post -Headers $headers -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"  # Create a PR $body = @{ title = "..."; head = "branch-name"; base = "main"; body = "..." } | ConvertTo-Json Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/pulls" -Method Post -Headers $headers -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"  # List open issues Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/issues?state=open" -Headers $headers  # Get a specific issue Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/issues/139" -Headers $headers

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Branch naming: <issue-number>-short-description (e.g. 137-fix-windows-adam-paths). work goes feature branches; PRs target main. Never push directly main. Commit messages: imperative mood, short (e.g. Fix #139: update cards_constructs.xlsx). Every PR must linked GitHub issue. Inform user editing siera source files issue exists yet.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: generated R scripts example scripts inst/script/ must always use current cards API. Key function renames cards ≥ 0.7.0: ard_categorical() → ard_tabulate() ard_continuous() → ard_summary() ard_complex() → ard_mvsummary() updating example scripts code templates, check cards changelog deprecations. strata argument exists ard_tabulate() (unlike old ard_categorical()) computing percentages observed combinations — see cards docs vs strata distinction. ard_tabulate() denominator pattern: categorical n(%) analyses pre-computed denominator, correct structure = 'group_var', variables = 'analysis_var' pre-counted denominator data frame count column renamed ...ard_N.... old pattern = c('group_var', 'analysis_var'), variables = 'dummy_var' raw data frame denominator longer correct. Example: cards API generated output actually comes : cards/cardx function names argument patterns generated (inst/script/) ARDs copied verbatim ARS metadata’s AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate.templateCode — siera substitutes placeholder parameters, rewrite cards calls. Consequence: bring generated scripts onto newer cards API editing siera’s R code vignettes alone; example metadata’s templateCode must updated. bundled Common_Safety_Displays_cards.xlsx carries templates AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate sheet (templateCode column, 5 rows). name-swap (ard_categorical→ard_tabulate, ard_continuous→ard_summary) runs fine current cards — ard_tabulate() still accepts legacy = c('grp','var'), variables = 'dummy', denominator = raw_df pattern, “old” denominator pattern still executes yields identical results even though ...ard_N... form preferred new templates. modify test fixture xlsx files inst/extdata/ without explicit owner authorisation — package owner normally updates separately. (PR #162 owner authorise renaming cards functions templateCode; edit made openxlsx::loadWorkbook() → writeData() single column → saveWorkbook(), preserves 24 sheets — verify sheet count re-run readARS() afterwards.) example R scripts inst/script/ pre-generated committed; regenerate manually using readARS() xlsx templates updated, gsub() ADaM-loading readr::read_csv('…/<DS>.csv' lines readr::read_csv(siera::ARS_example("<DS>.csv").
code: denom <- df_pop |> dplyr::count(TRT01A) |> dplyr::rename(`...ard_N...` = n) cards::ard_tabulate(data = in_data, by = 'TRT01A', variables = 'AGEGR1', denominator = denom)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Every code change must accompanied test. add feature, add test fail without . fix bug, add test reproduces bug. submit PR without new updated tests covering change. Integration tests source generated .R script isolated new.env(parent = baseenv()) inspect resulting ARD object. Long-running tests (source generated scripts real ADaM data) guarded skip_on_cran(). Temp directories use withr::local_tempdir() automatic cleanup. Example ARS files live inst/extdata/ (exampleARS_1 – exampleARS_6, .json .xlsx). Retrieve tests via ARS_example("exampleARS_6.json"). eTFL Portal integration testing — tests/testthat/testdata/etfl/ holds CDISC eTFL Portal fixtures unzipped three subfolders (.zip files committed): metadata/<table>-siera.json (ARS metadata enriched codeTemplate + parameters siera emits runnable scripts), adam/<table>/*.xpt (ADaM data table), reference/<table>-ard.json (published reference ARD). test-etfl-regression.R runs full pipeline 12 tables compares computed statistics reference ARD; machinery lives helper-etfl.R (.run_etfl_pipeline() reads XPT via haven::read_xpt() → CSV → readARS() → sources script; .load_etfl_reference(); .cmp_bigN/.cmp_n_pct/.cmp_n_pct_2level/.cmp_continuous/.cmp_rd comparators; .expect_all_match()). Reference ARDs Dataset-JSON; load jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyDataFrame=FALSE, simplifyVector=FALSE) avoid row-parsing failures. fda-ds-t04 derives DISCONFL DCTREAS via adsl_transform hook. Known-limitation analyses exercised asserted: 0-event risk difference returns NA (#156), per-term/per-category RD emitted (#157), top-N AE per-term subject counts can differ (#158). raw XPT/JSON ~28 MB working tree git-packs tarball-gzips ~2 MB, ships fine CRAN.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Keep documentation functionality sync. user-visible change — new feature, behaviour change, change generated-script ARD output, new capability user know — must reflected documentation (vignettes, README, NEWS.md) part change, deferred. Treat user-facing docs part definition done. doubt whether change user-visible, assume update docs. siera-docs-refresh skill automates full documentation pass. Five vignettes, ordered via number prefix %\VignetteIndexEntry{}: 1. Getting_started, 2. concepts (Concepts conventions), 3. using-cards, 4. ARD_script_structure, 5. apply-ARD. Keep prefixes sync _pkgdown.yml reordering. _pkgdown.yml groups Articles navbar (Get started / Concepts / Guides / Next steps). reference index intentionally left ungrouped. pipeline diagram hand-authored static SVG (CRAN-safe, new dependency), committed twice: vignettes/figures/siera-pipeline.svg (used concepts.Rmd via knitr::include_graphics()) man/figures/siera-pipeline.svg (used README / pkgdown home). Update copies together. Verifying vignettes without Pandoc: full rmarkdown::render() / build_vignettes() needs Pandoc (unavailable locally), knitr::knit(vig, output = tempfile()) executes chunks without Pandoc — use confirm chunks run. Getting_started.Rmd live chunk source()s inst/script/ example prints head(ARD), requires cards/cardx installed example scripts current. Tone conversational, second-person, tutorial-style inline-commented chunks; match editing docs. Audience clinical-trial practitioners, R developers. README.Rmd’s install chunks lack eval=FALSE, re-knitting actually run install.packages()/install_github(). small static edits, change README.Rmd README.md hand sync rather re-knitting.

Notify user strong candidates creating Claude skill — repeated prompts processes benefit automation. Target audience clinical trial industry members; whenever logic usage changes, suggest education material vignette idea. Inform user editing siera source files linked GitHub issue — changes traceable issue land feature branch. Suggest documentation updates features added functionality changes (public-facing CRAN package). Add tests new code; ensure every new branch test coverage changes. Ask clarifying questions check understanding uncertainty request approach.

what: My ARD program has been auto-generated: What can I expect?
title: ARD program structure
text: auto-generated ARD program (one generated output) follows logical structure linked ARS model. script contains code analyses related output, follows code pattern analysis (except first analysis, handles “big N” calculation convention). analysis-level ARD generated analysis, end program, analysis-level ARDs appended create one output-level ARD. Keep mind code sections auto-populated ARS metadata. can visualized follows:
code: # Section 1: Program header  # Section 2: Load libraries  # Section 3: Load ADaM datasets  # Section 4a (first Analysis): Code to calculate results as an ARD  # Section 4b (subsequent Analyses): Code to calculate results as an ARD  # Section 5: Append Analysis-level ARDs

title: ARD program structure
text: analysis related output follows logical structure based ARS model create analysis-level ARD. structure follows:

previous_headings: My ARD program has been auto-generated: What can I expect? > Analysis-level code to calculate ARDs
title: ARD program structure
text: step applies Analysis Set assigned output (e.g. Safety Population) ADaM dataset(s). case “big N” count based another dataset (like ADSL) main ADaM (e.g. ADAE), two separate datasets created downstream use subsequent analyses. Example: Note: done first Analysis, assigned subsequent analyses, since dataset(s) remain remainder program’s analyses.
code: overlap <- intersect(names(ADSL), names(ADAE)) overlapfin <- setdiff(overlap, "USUBJID")  df_pop <- dplyr::filter(   ADSL,   SAFFL == "Y" ) |>   merge(ADAE |> dplyr::select(-dplyr::all_of(overlapfin)),     by = "USUBJID",     all = FALSE   )  df_poptot <- dplyr::filter(   ADSL,   SAFFL == "Y" )

previous_headings: My ARD program has been auto-generated: What can I expect? > Analysis-level code to calculate ARDs
title: ARD program structure
text: Based resulting dataset step 1, data subsetting applied relevant current analysis (e.g. filtering serious, treatment-related Adverse Events). data subsetting required analysis, simple assignment previous dataset done ‘filter’ statement. step convention starting dataframe name “df2”, followed AnalysisId.
code: df2_An07_03_SerTEAE_Summ_ByTrt <- df_pop |>   dplyr::filter(TRTEMFL == "Y" & AESER == "Y")

previous_headings: My ARD program has been auto-generated: What can I expect? > Analysis-level code to calculate ARDs
title: ARD program structure
text: step takes subsetted dataset, applies required AnalysisMethod (e.g. counting subjects treatment group, like RACE). explained vignette using cards cardx, functions packages applied handle statistical operations analysis. Typically, pre-work done dataset passing cards cardx function. function applied, result analysis-level ARD. end step, record-level metadata ARS model also merged ARD, ensure ability trace result back ARS metadata. See example : example uses current cards function name ard_tabulate() (formerly ard_categorical()); see using cards cardx vignette full list renames. final mutate() siera stamps CDISC ARD traceability columns onto every row. grouping applied analysis see group[n]_groupingId (grouping column belongs ) plus one : group[n]_groupId - pre-defined groups (groups listed metadata, like treatment arms), mapped group[n]_level via case_when(), ; group[n]_groupValue - data-driven groupings (dataDriven: true, categories cause death discovered ADaM data run time), capturing discovered value directly. See Concepts conventions vignette full picture traceability columns.
code: # intermediate step: Prepare Denominator Dataset for `cards` function denom_dataset <- df2_An01_05_SAF_Summ_ByTrt |>   dplyr::select(TRT01A)  # intermediate step: Prepare input dataset for `cards` function in_data <- df2_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt |>   dplyr::distinct(TRT01A, RACE, USUBJID) |>   dplyr::mutate(dummy = "dummyvar")  # calculate subject counts and % (based on big N) grouped by treatment and race df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt <- cards::ard_tabulate(   data = in_data,   by = c("TRT01A", "RACE"),   variables = "dummy",   denominator = denom_dataset )  # select relevant statistics as defined by the Method, and assign operation Ids df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt <- df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt |>   dplyr::filter(stat_name %in% c("n", "p")) |>   dplyr::mutate(operationid = dplyr::case_when(     stat_name == "n" ~ "Mth01_1_n",     stat_name == "p" ~ "Mth01_2_pct"   ))  # stamp CDISC ARD traceability metadata so each result traces back to the ARS definition df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt <- df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt |>   dplyr::mutate(     AnalysisId = "An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt",     MethodId = "Mth01",     OutputId = "Out14-1-1",     # the grouping this column belongs to (here: the treatment-arm grouping)     group1_groupingId = "AnlsGrouping_01_Trt",     # for pre-defined groups, map each observed level to its ARS group id     group1_groupId = dplyr::case_when(       as.character(group1_level) == "Placebo"              ~ "AnlsGrouping_01_Trt_1",       as.character(group1_level) == "Xanomeline Low Dose"  ~ "AnlsGrouping_01_Trt_2",       as.character(group1_level) == "Xanomeline High Dose" ~ "AnlsGrouping_01_Trt_3"     )   )

title: ARD program structure
text: process repeats Analysis, although code step course vary (defined specific ARS metadata Analysis). Analysis ARD created, ARDs appened create output-level ARD. See example :
code: # combine analyses to create ARD ---- ARD <- dplyr::bind_rows(   df3_An01_05_SAF_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_01_Age_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_01_Age_Comp_ByTrt,   df3_An03_02_AgeGrp_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_02_AgeGrp_Comp_ByTrt,   df3_An03_03_Sex_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_03_Sex_Comp_ByTrt,   df3_An03_04_Ethnic_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_04_Ethnic_Comp_ByTrt,   df3_An03_05_Race_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_05_Race_Comp_ByTrt,   df3_An03_06_Height_Summ_ByTrt,   df3_An03_06_Height_Comp_ByTrt )

title: ARD program structure
text: pattern scales without special handling part: Arbitrarily deep table hierarchies - table stub (defined mainListOfContents) can nest deeply output requires; longer three-level limit. three grouping factors - analysis can split four groupings (e.g. Treatment x Age group x Sex x Region). grouping simply adds group[n]_* set columns ARD.

title: Getting Started
text: start, helps see headed. end product siera Analysis Results Dataset (ARD) - one result per row, machine-readable form. ARD Demographics output, produced running one example scripts siera generates (running scripts ). building toward: row carries statistic together metadata (AnalysisId grouping columns) traces back ARS definition. Concepts conventions vignette explains traceability columns detail.
code: # Run a ready-made example script shipped with siera; the result is the object `ARD` source(ARD_script_example("ARD_Out14-1-1.R")) head(ARD) #>   group1 group1_level group2 group2_level variable variable_level stat_name #> 1   <NA>           NA   <NA>           NA   TRT01A        Placebo         n #> 2   <NA>           NA   <NA>           NA   TRT01A      Xanomeli…         n #> 3   <NA>           NA   <NA>           NA   TRT01A      Xanomeli…         n #> 4 TRT01A      Placebo   <NA>           NA      AGE             NA         N #> 5 TRT01A      Placebo   <NA>           NA      AGE             NA      mean #> 6 TRT01A      Placebo   <NA>           NA      AGE             NA        sd #>   stat_label   stat #> 1          n     86 #> 2          n     84 #> 3          n     84 #> 4          N     86 #> 5       Mean 75.209 #> 6         SD   8.59 # To see a list of example files: ARS_example() #>  [1] "ADAE.csv"                          "ADEXSUM.csv"                       #>  [3] "ADSL.csv"                          "ADVS.csv"                          #>  [5] "ADZSDER.csv"                       "cards_constructs.xlsx"             #>  [7] "Common_Safety_Displays_cards.xlsx" "exampleARS_1.json"                 #>  [9] "exampleARS_1a.json"                "exampleARS_2.json"                 #> [11] "exampleARS_2.xlsx"                 "exampleARS_2a.xlsx"                #> [13] "exampleARS_3.json"                 "exampleARS_3.xlsx"                 #> [15] "exampleARS_4.json"                 "exampleARS_5.json"                 #> [17] "exampleARS_5.xlsx"                 "exampleARS_6.json"                 #> [19] "exampleARS_6.xlsx"                 "test_cards.json"  # A temporary path to a specific file: ARS_example("exampleARS_1.json") #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/siera/extdata/exampleARS_1.json"

what: Reading in ARS metadata - what to expect
title: Getting Started
text: ARS metadata contains ARS information Reporting Event. information includes: mainListOfContents (links outputs respective analyses) otherListsOfContents (contains Output metadata) dataSubsets (filters data individual analyses) analysisSets (filters data get Population Set Output) analysisGroupings (groups data according analysis specifications) analyses (contains linking information components required calculation results) methods (describes operations performed data get results) fuller treatment sections , flow siera, see Concepts conventions vignette.

title: Getting Started
text: readARS function reads completed ARS metadata file, generates R scripts Output defined file. example can done using ready--use ARS metadata Excel file (note meant example. Official ARS metadata json format, can also passed readARS function): now 5 R scripts (named ARD_Out14-1-1.R, ARD_Out14-3-1-1.R, ARD_Out14-3-2-1.R, ARD_Out14-3-3-1a.R, ARD_Out14-3-3-1b.R) folder specified output_folder. can execute 5 R scripts -(assuming ADaM required script available ADaM_folder), result ARD (one result per row format) scripts. generated scripts portable across operating systems: Windows-style backslashes ADaM path normalised forward slashes, script generated Windows runs unchanged macOS Linux (vice versa).
code: # Path to the Excel ARS metadata file: ARS_path <- ARS_example("Common_Safety_Displays_cards.xlsx")  # Path to a folder which will contain the Output meta-programmed R scripts (recommended to update # to a more suitable local path) output_folder <- tempdir()  # Path to the folder containing ADaM datasets in csv format (we will use temporary # directory tempdir() to make the code run in this vignette, but it's recommended to # 1. download the ADaMs required (csv ADSL and ADAE available using e.g. ARS_example("ADSL.csv")) # 2. store it in a folder (the directly downloaded location can be found using dirname(ARS_example("ADSL.csv")), for example.  Use this location, or manually store the ADaM somewhere else) # 3. use the folder path instead of tempdir() below. ADaM_folder <- tempdir()  # run the readARS function with these 3 parameters.  This creates R scripts # (1 for each Output in output_folder) readARS(ARS_path, output_folder, ADaM_folder)

title: Getting Started
text: structure auto-generated ARD script discussed ARD structure vignette. Examples auto-generated ARD scripts included package, can accessed ARD_script_example function. Running one (produce preview top vignette) looks like : ARD object result appending analysis-level ARD (created cards cardx functions) output. can used (re-used) downstream various applications. See vignette next step utilising ARD - like creating Tables, using QC.
code: # Step 1: open ARD_xxx.R file # Step 2: Confirm the location of ADaM dataset(s) is correct in the code section "Load ADaM". # For the sake of simplicity, the only update made to the ARD_Out14-1-1.R script was to point to the ADaM folder to ARS_example("ADSL.csv") # Step 3: Run the code and enjoy automated analysis results generation. # Note: the ARD is contained in the object "ARD" (which contains the appended) mini-ARDs from all individual Analyses ARDs.  example_ARD_script <- ARD_script_example("ARD_Out14-1-1.R") source(example_ARD_script) head(ARD)

title: What do I do with my ARD?
text: Analysis Results Dataset (ARD) multiple uses. Consisting machine-readable results (opposed static RTF PDF results), examples ARD use cases : QC datasets re-using results different outputs, e.g. figures tables re-using results various reporting events creating final TFLs (Concepts conventions vignette discusses ARD makes use cases easier.)

what: Downstream R packages for table creation
title: What do I do with my ARD?
text: Two reputable R packages, part pharmaverse ARD-first approach creating tables: tfrmt gtsummary presentations delivered posit::conf 2025 packages: tfrmt gtsummary

title: Concepts and conventions
text: Traditionally, analysis results lived inside static outputs - numbers RTF PDF table. Analysis Results Dataset (ARD) instead stores results machine-readable data, one row per result, metadata describing exactly number . results data, several things become lot easier: QC - results can compared programmatically independent calculation, instead eye. Re-use across outputs - result can feed table, figure, listing without re-calculated. Re-use across reporting events - results computed can carried later submission different deliverable. Creating final TFLs - downstream packages tfrmt gtsummary format ARDs straight submission-ready tables. siera’s job get ARD without writing analysis code hand: supply ARS metadata, siera writes R produces ARD.

title: Concepts and conventions
text: flow always : start ARS metadata (Analysis Results Standard description reporting event), either JSON Excel format. pass readARS(), writes one R script per Output defined metadata. run generated script ADaM datasets, result ARD - one row per result, ready downstream use. statistical computation performed cards cardx packages, whose functions siera writes generated scripts (see vignette using cards cardx).

what: The seven ARS sections siera consumes
title: Concepts and conventions
text: ARS metadata describes whole reporting event, siera needs seven sections generate code. worth knowing one contributes: generated script assembled pieces, every result produces carries identifiers back (see “Reading ARD row” ).

title: Concepts and conventions
text: ARS metadata officially travels JSON, siera also accepts Excel (XLSX) representation information. two semantically equivalent - readARS() produces generated scripts either way, can choose whichever format fits tooling. examples shipped package include (see ARS_example()).

what: Reading an ARD row: CDISC traceability columns
title: Concepts and conventions
text: core promise ARD traceability: every result can traced back metadata defines . make possible, row siera-generated ARD carries identifier columns alongside statistic : AnalysisId - analysis produced row. operationid - operation within method (e.g. n count vs. %). group[n]_groupingId - grouping column belongs (e.g. treatment-arm grouping). group[n]_groupId - pre-defined groups (groups listed explicitly metadata), identifier specific group. group[n]_groupValue - data-driven groupings (dataDriven: true, categories discovered ADaM data run time, e.g. cause death AE term), actual value found data. distinction matters: treatment-arm grouping usually pre-defined, rows carry group1_groupId; grouping “cause death” typically data-driven, rows carry group1_groupValue whatever categories appeared data. single ARD can contain . ARD program structure vignette shows columns stamped generated code.

title: Making use of `cards` and `cardx`
text: siera package, R code used AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate aligned cards cardx package. simple example function ard_summary cards package used AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate class: note cards function names. Recent versions cards renamed several functions: ard_continuous() now ard_summary(), ard_categorical() now ard_tabulate(), ard_complex() now ard_mvsummary(). siera’s example metadata generated scripts use current names; reading scripts produced older siera version may still see previous names. example AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate code particular AnalysisMethod (summary statistics continuous variable), ard_summary function cards package used. Notice, however, two parameters used code: ‘byvariables_here’, ‘analysisvariable_here’. example placeholder names, replaced actual variables, depending context Analysis. example, ‘byvariables_here’ replaced TRT01A, ‘analysisvariable_here’ replaced AGE, ARD summary statistics AGE Treatment. code, however, reused summary statistics e.g. HEIGHT another Analysis, ‘analysisvariable_here’ replaced HEIGHT. dynamically applied within readARS() function generating R scripts using siera. Let’s see, example, statement auto-populated actual objects (ARS metadata): order facilitate use placeholders replacement actual values metadata, several “constructs” defined readARS function, available user call AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate AnalysisMethodCodeParameters classes metadata. constructs either simple variables datasets defined metadata, complex, dynamic constructs various metadata pieces, inserted specific cards functions, pre-processing steps. list defined constructs examples shipped package, can accessed :
code: # example of 'cards' code in AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate, using the ard_summary function:  Analysis_ARD <- ard_summary(   data = filtered_ADSL,   by = c(byvariables_here),   variables = analysisvariable_here ) # example of 'cards' code in AnalysisMethodCodeTemplate, populated with AnalysisMethodCodeParameters:  Analysis_ARD <- ard_summary(   data = filtered_ADSL,   by = c(TRT01A),   variables = AGE ) ARS_example("cards_constructs.xlsx")

title: Generate Analysis Results Programmes Using ARS Metadata
text: siera, users ingest Analysis Results Standard (ARS) metadata auto-generate R scripts , run corresponding ADaM datasets, provide Analysis Results Datasets (ARDs). CDISC Analysis Results Standard foundational standard facilitates automation, reproducibility, reusability traceability analysis results data. ARS metadata officially represented using JSON format (though also Excel representation easier readability, JSON format recommended official ARS usage). JSON file contains relevant metadata able calculate Analysis Results specific Reporting Event. metadata includes (limited ): Analysis Sets (e.g. SAFFL = “Y”) AnalysisGroupings (e.g. group Treatment) DataSubsets (e.g. filter Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events) AnalysisMethods (e.g. calculate ‘n’, ‘Mean’, ‘Min’, ‘Max’, ‘Q1’, ‘Q3’) Applying concepts ADaM input data, yields Analysis Results Dataset format (ARDs). Within pharmaverse ecosystem, siera sits ARS metadata cards package: ADaM datasets typically built admiral, siera generates cards/cardx code computes ARD, packages gtsummary tfrmt turn ARD final tables.

title: Generate Analysis Results Programmes Using ARS Metadata

code: install.packages("siera") devtools::install_github("clymbclinical/siera")

One R script created output (table) defined ARS metadata reportingg event. order make use function, following required arguments: functional ARS file, representing ARS Metadata Reporting Event (JSON xlsx) output directory R scripts placed folder containing related ADaM datasets ARDs generated See Getting Started vignette examples detail process.

title: Ingest ARS (Analysis Results Standard) metadata, produce ARD (Analysis Results Dataset) code for each output — readARS
text: ARS_path file containing ARS metadata reporting event output_path Path store .R ARD scripts adam_path Path folder containing ADaM datasets, run ARD program spec_output output ID specific output run metadata output_format Format emitting generated ARD. Must exactly one `"none"` `"datasetjson"` (partial matching). `"none"` (default) generates ARD scripts . `"datasetjson"` additionally appends code writes ARD CDISC Dataset-JSON file (`ARD_<OutputId>.json`) generated script run. Dataset-JSON export requires optional datasetjson package installed environment runs generated script.

title: Ingest ARS (Analysis Results Standard) metadata, produce ARD (Analysis Results Dataset) code for each output — readARS
text: R programmes generating ARDs - one output (analysis output) specified ARS metadata

code: # path to file containing ARS metadata  ARS_path <- ARS_example("Common_Safety_Displays_cards.xlsx")  # output path for R programs output_dir <- tempdir()  # folder containing ADaM datasets adam_folder <- tempdir()  # run function, write to temp directory readARS(ARS_path, output_dir, adam_folder) #> New names: #> • `` -> `...11` #> • `` -> `...12` #> • `` -> `...13`

title: siera 0.5.6
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-17 Fixed readARS() crash ARS metadata contains referencedAnalysisOperations (continuous-tables shift tables) ensuring accumulator frames initialised merge key columns. Normalised Windows backslashes ADaM file paths forward slashes generated scripts run correctly Windows Unix systems. Added CDISC ARD-compliant column stamping: generated scripts now write group[n]_groupingId, group[n]_groupId (pre-defined groups) group[n]_groupValue (data-driven groupings) onto ARD row. Supported arbitrarily deep nesting mainListOfContents via recursive LOPA extraction helper, removing previous three-level hard limit. Expanded support multi-value ARS group conditions (/NOTIN) unnesting condition.value list columns JSON parser. Coerced *_level columns character individual ARD frame bind_rows, preventing type conflicts continuous categorical analyses. Allowed three grouping factors per analysis. Updated bundled cards_constructs.xlsx example file extended test coverage across metadata parsing, ADaM loading, ARD script generation.

title: siera 0.5.5
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-01 Strengthened analysis method validation clear errors missing undefined MethodId values warnings method templates parameter value sources resolved. Added guardrails analysis set generation warn missing incomplete metadata fall back unfiltered analysis dataset needed. Tightened ARS metadata ingestion requiring analysisSets definitions JSON analysis code template sheets Excel workbooks. Expanded test coverage analysis methods, analysis sets, data subset condition handling capture metadata edge cases.

title: siera 0.5.4
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-25 Comprehensive testing added: checking generated ARD result values added warning messages cli() cover scenarios bigN vs table-level subsetting

title: siera 0.5.3
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-28 Cover scenario multi-value DataSubsets Cover scenario overlapping variables ADSL merge

text: CRAN release: 2025-08-25 Added conditional operators cover EQ, NE, GE, GT, LE, LT
